How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-06-22 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 Flags tickers appearing across 2+ coherent screeners within one trading day, ranking validated combos (SMART_FLOW x UNUSUAL_V3, 3+ VALID) highest by score.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →
Validated & debunked combos — numbers, not vibes
ComboData confidenceBacktestRead

Generic confluence carries the bucket-level stats only (3+ VALID screeners: WR 81.7% at +20d). A pair NOT listed above has no proven pair-level edge - treat the overlap as attention, not size. New-screener pairs re-enter the battery as their history grows.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

PANW dominates at 529, a clear score outlier hitting CLENOW_MOMENTUM, PULLBACK, and VOLUME_LEADER simultaneously - the gap to second place is large enough to treat it as the only high-conviction name. AMAT lands second at 194 on the same momentum-plus-volume combination with block trader confirmation, a cleaner institutional fingerprint. No ticker in today's batch matched a validated combo, meaning every name is running on raw screener overlap without the backtested edge of SMART_FLOW x UNUSUAL_V3 or the 3+ VALID cluster (WR 81.7%, Sharpe 2.68) - that materially caps conviction across the board. The score distribution is sharply top-heavy with a 335-point gap between first and second, and the rest of the field bunches between 76 and 169. NEUTRAL macro at 51.95 adds no tailwind. Take PANW first on the score gap and momentum confirmation, but size it as a single-screener-quality trade, not a validated-combo position.

BLOWOFF_DETECTOR
🧠 Spots parabolic tops about to roll over on the top-sector winners — Greenwood-Shleifer-You 2-year rallies, Edwards-Magee parabolic, gap exhaustion, island reversals and LPPLS variance acceleration. Read
Top blow-off candidate today: LRCX (score 8, target ~$349.14 (DD20d -11.6%)).

3 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 2 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.

🧠 Detects institutional options sweeps with next-day open-interest confirmation, EMA trend filter, and low-OI anomaly to isolate informed directional flow.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $TLT $COIN
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1TLT ↑55 Watch$86.19 +0.11%BULLISH SWEEPCALL75$605KNEUTRAL0100STRONG1111
2COIN ↑50 Watch$159.02 -3.53%BULLISH SWEEPCALL182.5$225KOPENING138876BROKEN44
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking has only 5 resolved trades against a 280-signal universe, far short of the 20 needed for any verdict, so the 100% win rate and 200% average return tell you nothing about the backtest's 68.1% win rate and 3.84% mean PnL - the key number is 5, and it makes the live record statistically void.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
HALF SIZEinsufficient data (5 resolved, need ≥20)
Last 90 days: 282 signals captured · 5 resolved at +20d · WR 100% · avg +200.0% · vs SPY +4.6%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 280 closed · WR TB 100% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is a single name: COIN at score 50, Watch tier, flagged on a bullish sweep. Score 50 sits at the floor of the Watch band, well below the 65 and 80 thresholds where the screener's edge concentrates, and zero signals cleared either bar. With only one signal and a median equal to the max, this is a degenerate distribution, not a real batch. The neutral macro regime (score 51.95) adds no tailwind. Conviction here is low; COIN is the only option but treat it as a monitor-only position until the score firms above 65 on a subsequent sweep confirmation.

🧠 Identifies stocks where IV30 exceeds HV30 by a statistically meaningful margin, signaling overpriced implied vol and a premium-selling edge, adjusted for sector and value factors.
Construction
IV30 vs HV30 spread + sector bias (Sinclair Table 4.7: Utilities +31%, Tech -8%) + value factors (P/E, RoE, mcap). Score 0-100 ranks premium-selling candidates.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape confirms the edge: 59% win rate on 469 resolved trades, avg +4.6% vs SPY +1.2%, a clean +3.3pt spread - but the Watch tier (+6.0% avg) outrunning the Signal tier (+2.3% avg) contradicts the expected tier hierarchy and is the one number worth watching before leaning hard on Signal names.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

NEE and ES share the top score of 76, both utilities exploiting Sinclair's documented +31% utility sector variance premium bias, making them the structural favorites in today's batch. PM at 75 adds a defensive consumer angle with similarly stable realized vol. The distribution is narrow and compressed - median 68, max only 76, zero names above 80, with 40 of 44 above 65 - so this is a clustered, low-ceiling batch with no standout conviction names. NEUTRAL macro (51.95) is an uninspiring backdrop for short-vol strategies but not a veto. Take NEE first at low size, the utility sector tailwind is the strongest structural argument here and the score edge over the rest is thin.

Longs tickers in the MID tertile of the variance risk premium (IV vs realized): options priced coherently with realized vol, neither too cheap (LOW = market disbelief) nor too rich (HIGH = catalyst already priced).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

AMTM at 100 and JD at 99 lead the batch at Very Strong tier, though neither carries a pattern tag, leaving the directional catalyst rationale thin. SCHD at 97 is notable as an ETF in this screener, signaling that broad dividend equity sits in the coherent-pricing sweet spot today, more useful as a macro read than a single-name trade. The distribution is moderately top-heavy: 13 of 38 signals score 80 or above against a median of 69, so there is a real upper cluster without the whole batch being artificially bunched. NEUTRAL macro at 51.95 neither helps nor hurts, and the screener is designed for calm-to-drifting tape rather than high-velocity regimes, which fits the current setup adequately. First preference is AMTM on score, at moderate conviction only, given the full absence of pattern context across the top eight names.

🧠 Detects put volume spiking 3x the 30-day average in stocks with stable or rising price, betting institutional hedging in calm tape precedes upside continuation via IV crush.
Construction
Put volume > 3× avg(30d) + price stable or up. Kerberos thesis: contrarian - institutional hedging activity in calm tape often precedes upside continuation. Score weighs put vol ratio, price stability, IV crush.

#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPut volPut ratioPut premium
1SIMO ↑100●●● Very strong$309.17 -8.23%27165.57$17308K

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution makes ranking impossible: all 50 signals print 100, median is 100, and every name clears both the 80 and 65 thresholds, meaning the scorer is ceiling-pegged with zero differentiation. Among the eight shown, ADI (Analog Devices) is the only name with options market depth large enough to confirm genuine institutional put flow rather than noise, making it the only defensible starting point. AAOI adds an optical networking angle relevant to current semis tape, and ARMK is a lower-volatility services name where a put surge is harder to explain and therefore slightly more interesting. Pattern data is null across the board, so there is no structural confirmation beyond the raw flow signal. Macro at NEUTRAL (51.95) neither amplifies nor kills the thesis, and with a ceiling-pegged batch this weak on differentiation, conviction is low across all names - ADI first, small size, only if you can verify the put flow is not earnings-driven.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape confirms the backtest edge is real - 66.3% win rate and avg +2.5% beats SPY by +1.8 percentage points - but the monthly cohort trend is deteriorating, with May 2026 dropping to 49.7% WR and +0.94% avg after the March peak of 67% WR and +4.9%, which is a mild contradiction to the backtest's 65% long-run win rate and signals the strategy may be losing edge as the tape softens.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

RILY prints a perfect 100 with a LAUNCH pattern, making it the mechanical leader of today's batch, though its fundamentals are troubled enough to warrant position sizing caution. ACRS and MGNX both score 95 in the same LAUNCH configuration, giving the biotech sleeve an unusual concentration worth watching. The score distribution is extreme: all 50 signals sit at 80 or above with a median of 85, meaning there is zero dispersion and no lower-conviction names diluting the set. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (51.95), providing neither a structural tailwind nor a headwind for this reversal strategy. The critical caveat is that the Very Strong tier - which owns every name in today's batch - shows only 50% live win rate and avg return of -0.10% across 4 resolved trades, the worst tier outcome in the 90-day tracking table. Take RILY first at half-Kelly given that tier-level live underperformance, and keep position sizes tight until Very Strong resolves at a better rate.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The batch is top-heavy in the best possible way: all 37 signals score 80 or above, with 8 names tied at the 94 maximum - APPN, G, HUBS, KVYO, MNDY, NIO, NOW, and UPWK. No pattern data is available for any of them, so entry timing leans on score alone rather than a discrete setup. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 51.95, which neither accelerates nor kills a mean-reversion basket, though it offers no tailwind. This screener is designed as a basket play (1-2% per name, LEAPS), not a single-name trigger, so treat the 94-score cluster as a construction slate rather than a ranked pick list. First priority is HUBS and NOW given their liquidity depth for LEAPS positioning, at moderate conviction pending pattern confirmation.

🧠 Detects vol-of-vol climax via VVIX percentile, VIX term structure, FOMC proximity, and weekend effect to flag sell-volatility entry points.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑63 Watch$733.64 -1.44%SELL VOL1.55
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 Detects stocks with options volume exceeding 5x their 20-day mean, filtered for calm price action, to front-run institutional positioning before a catalyst.
Construction
Stocks with options volume > 5× mean(20d) + premium > $50K + Vol/OI > 5. V3 filters now applied at write time: keeps only calm (vol<1.5× avg) and non-extended (±5% from EMA20) tickers, drops LATE-timing chasing signals.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 AI read · today's batch

OWL leads the batch at 71 (Signal tier) but its pattern is a BEARISH SWEEP flagged as a LONG direction, a contradiction that cuts conviction in half. RUN at 68 carries the same conflict, bearish sweep mapped to a long bias, making the intent of the flow ambiguous. COIN at 62 (Watch) is the cleanest name today, bullish sweep aligned with a long direction and no directional conflict. The score distribution is weak, max 71, median 58, zero names above 80 and only two above 65, so this is a clustered, low-conviction batch with nothing standing out. NEUTRAL macro at 51.95 does not argue for stretching on ambiguous setups. First look is COIN at Watch-tier conviction only, sized accordingly, with OWL and RUN on hold until the sweep direction is reconciled.

Construction
Classifies 11 GICS sector ETFs into 8 categories: EARLY_ENTRY, RIDING, LATE_CHASE, EXHAUSTED, DECELERATING, NEW_DOWN, DEAD, NEUTRAL. Uses strength + acceleration + RS crossover vs SPY.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XLU is the standout at 95 (Very Strong), which is worth taking seriously on its own terms but is also a flag: defensives leading a momentum screener in a NEUTRAL macro regime (score 51.95) is a mixed signal, not a clean setup. XLC and XLE both score 80 (Strong) and represent a more macro-consistent pair if the tape is rotating toward real-asset and communication names. The score distribution is top-heavy, with 4 of 11 signals at 80 or above and the median at 60, but five names cluster at 50 to 60 with no pattern data attached, making the lower half of the batch uninformative. No pattern field is populated for any name, which removes a key filter the construction promises. XLU first in size given its score, XLC second, both at moderate conviction only - the macro NEUTRAL and missing pattern data cap confidence across the board.

🧠 Detects stocks down 40-plus percent from 52-week highs that show capitulation volume with a reversal candle, targeting mean reversion within 15 days.
Construction
Deep oversold (≥-40% from 52w high) with capitulation volume + reversal candle. Score combines drawdown depth, ATR contraction, volume spike, and proximity to 52w low. ATR-based stop 2.5×, target 3.0×, max hold 15 days.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The tape contradicts the backtest sharply: live win rate of 46.2% runs 17 points below the backtested 63.3%, though the positive avg of plus 2.7% versus SPY plus 0.9% offers partial comfort, and monthly cohorts have improved steadily from January's minus 9.4% average to May's plus 4.6%, suggesting the screener works better in recovering markets than in drawdown regimes.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

WW at score 100 (Very strong, BREAKOUT) is the cleanest setup in today's batch, followed by ADCT at 95 and BYRN at 93, all three sharing the same BREAKOUT pattern that the live Very strong tier has averaged plus 30% on (n=8, so treat that with appropriate skepticism). The 50-signal batch is top-heavy: all 50 clear the 65 threshold and 19 score 80 or above, with median at 75, meaning there is genuine compression at the high end rather than a dispersed field. NEUTRAL macro at 51.95 is neither a tailwind nor a headwind, but note that the live Strong tier (where APTV, CELH, and CVNA sit) has posted a 28.6% win rate and negative 8.2% average in resolved trades, making those three names lower conviction despite their 85 scores. Take WW first at high conviction, size conservatively given the marginal backtest status, and skip the Strong-tier TRIGGER names until live performance in that tier repairs.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Ranks stocks by annualized exponential-regression slope times R-squared over 90 days, filtered above MA100 with a live SPY-above-MA200 regime gate.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

EOD
Data confidence HALF SIZE doctrine 8-step: MARGINAL at h=5d (Sharpe 0.5-0.6), REJECT at h>=10d; 60d academic drift not reproduced Full methodology →
🧠 Captures post-earnings price continuation when a positive EPS surprise, a gap up, and gap-hold combine with analyst revision momentum to sustain buying pressure.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.
Top picks: $BBCP $BKE
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySurpriseConsistencyPost-earn ret
1BBCP ↑90●●● Very strong$11.07 -1.69%500.0%437.6%
2BKE ↑70● Signal$44.50 -0.71%24.7%4-3.7%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape - WR 63%, avg +4.8% against SPY at +0.3%, a +4.5-point spread on 467 resolved trades - confirms the screener earns its KEEP verdict and meaningfully outperforms the MARGINAL backtest Sharpe of 0.5-0.6, with the May 2026 cohort (n=593, WR 61%, avg +4.5%) corroborating the trend, though the April cohort (n=6, 50% WR) is too small to be meaningful.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

CIEN and HPE are the only two names in today's batch, both scoring a perfect 100 at the 'Very strong' tier with LONG direction and no pattern data attached. Two signals at max score with a median of 100 means the batch is as thin as it gets, not a strong field compressed to the top but simply a near-empty screen. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (51.95), providing no tailwind or headwind. The critical problem is that the 'Very strong' tier - exactly the bucket both names occupy - has produced a negative average return of -0.71% across three resolved 90-day trades, so the tier label is actively misleading here. If forced to act, CIEN gets first look over HPE on no fundamental basis from this screen alone, but conviction is low and position sizing should reflect that.

Data confidence HALF SIZE backtest_murphy_intermarket doctrine 8-step: 13 buckets MARGINAL_INVERSE Bonferroni-significant (+6 to +14% SPY at 60-120d after intermarket correlation breaks) Full methodology →
🧠 Fires a contrarian LONG on SPY when two or more major intermarket pair correlations break by at least 2 sigma over seven days, exploiting cross-asset mean-reversion after dislocated relationships.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape - 65% win rate, +7.5% average return, beating SPY by 6.8 points on 43 resolved trades - confirms the screener's OBSERVATION-grade edge is real and earns a KEEP, but the tier split is the key number: the 'Signal' tier prints +14.97% average versus +4.43% for 'Very strong,' meaning today's all-'Very strong' batch sits in the worst-performing bucket of a generally positive history.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The batch of 50 signals is fully score-compressed: every name prints 100, median 100, all 50 above 80, so the screener's own ranking offers zero differentiation today. From the 8 named picks, BLD, AMBA, and AGCO are the most recognizable cross-sector representations of the signal, but all three sit in the 'Very strong' tier with null pattern data, which is the weakest-performing tier in live tracking at 45.8% win rate and +4.4% average - well below the 'Strong' and 'Signal' cohorts. NEUTRAL macro at 51.95 provides no directional lift. Given tier drag, score compression, and missing pattern context, this batch reads thin; if one name is worth touching first, BLD at reduced size, with no urgency to act before pattern context appears.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (WR 64.4%, avg +8.2%, beating SPY by 7.1 pts) confirms a real edge in resolved signals, but the monthly cohort trend complicates confidence: April printed a negative average (-0.73%) before May recovered to +3.98%, showing the edge is regime-sensitive and the 7.1-pt SPY beat is not a smooth constant.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

AI and BEAM both score 90 (Very strong, PRIMED) and are the lead names: AI carries high-profile speculative short interest in a volatile sector, while BEAM adds binary biotech catalyst risk that accelerates squeeze dynamics when positioning unwinds. CPB at 90 is the outlier worth watching as a consumer staple with a squeeze setup, which is an unusual combination that can move fast when it does move. The score distribution is top-heavy but not thin: 17 of 50 signals clear 80, median sits at 70, and 45 of 50 clear 65, so the batch has density in the upper band without being a one-name event. Macro prints NEUTRAL at 51.95, offering no directional assist to squeeze plays, which argues for measured sizing. Start with AI at first priority given score, sector vol, and short interest dynamics, but treat it as a tactical trade with predefined exit, not a thesis hold.

🧠 Captures stocks with surging volume relative to 10/30/90-day averages combined with positive price momentum across 1/5/20/90-day horizons, ranking the strongest volume-momentum leaders in the RUT-1000.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is entirely compressed: all 50 signals score 96 or above, median at 96, with every name in the batch above 80 - that compression strips the ranking of any real discrimination today. SMCI (100) and MRVL (99) are the most structurally interesting given their AI-infrastructure positioning and the fact that semis are driving the bulk of the volume leadership cluster alongside CRDO (99) and KLAC (99). CWAN (100) and SYRE (100) are in the top tier by score but lack any pattern context, making them harder to size. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (51.95), which neither endorses nor penalizes momentum names. With no pattern data on any pick and a completely undifferentiated score stack, preference goes to SMCI first on the basis of liquidity and sector relevance, at low conviction only - the screener cannot tell you who is actually leading today.

🧠 Detects institutional accumulation via large OTM options block trades on low-volume stocks, confirmed by next-day OI delta to distinguish new positioning from closing trades.
Construction
Large options block trades (>$200K premium, 1-10 DTE, OTM) on quiet stocks. Confirms next-day with OI delta to verify NEW position vs closing. Score weights total premium, vol/OI ratio, OI confirmation.

34NXTE ↑50 Watch$54.37 +1.51%1.872$0.3M
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape confirms rather than contradicts the observational backtest signal: 61.3% win rate and a +4.6% average 20-day return beat SPY by +3.6 points, and the monthly cohorts for April and May hold at 54-56% win rate with positive average returns, though the time-barred options win rate of 41.2% with 26% expired is the key number reminding you the edge is in the underlying move, not necessarily in the options themselves.

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 457 closed · WR TB 41% · 26% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

WDCX leads the batch at a score of 86, the only name at the top of the Strong tier, with SMQ close behind at 82. KW and VSCO both print 81, also Strong, making four names above 80 in a 34-signal batch where the median sits at 61, so the distribution is moderately top-heavy with a clear separation between the top cluster and the rest. No pattern context is available for any pick today, which leaves the thesis resting purely on premium size and OI confirmation rather than any structural setup. Macro is NEUTRAL at 51.95, neither a tailwind nor a headwind for this type of flow-driven signal. WDCX first at best conviction given the score lead, with SMQ as the secondary; size both conservatively until pattern data populates.

Data confidence STARTER PEAD gap-up + weekly trigger, small N, mechanically sound but too early for a verdict Full methodology →
🧠 Captures post-earnings drift by entering when a weekly red-candle pullback within four weeks of an earnings gap closes back above prior-week highs.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.
Top picks: $AAOX $ARMW $AMBQ
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayGapVol ratioBars ago

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Opening gap on one of the last 3 bars: 5.0% ≤ gap < 10.0%
  • Volume confirmation: gap-day volume / 20d average > 1.5
  • Dollar-volume ≥ $50M
Score
gap_pct × 15 (not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled, top pick = 100). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

Seven signals with a median of 73 and only two names above 80 makes this a top-heavy batch rather than a broad sweep of quality setups. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 51.95 is neither a tailwind nor a headwind for momentum continuation. ARMW is the first call at moderate conviction; hold AAOX to secondary unless it shows early relative strength.

Data confidence STARTER backtest_dalton_excess 2026-04 (500 tickers 2022-2026): raw excess = coin flip (WR 51%); WITH extreme-location + tail>=1.8xATR filter +3.18% @10d, N=74 only, no block bootstrap Full methodology →
🧠 Detects institutional excess at Market Profile value-area extremes via rejection tails >= 1.8x ATR, signaling mean reversion back into value.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.
Top picks: $E $NTST
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %Kerberos trap
1E ↑100●●● Very strong$49.16 -0.49%LOW3.3526.5%·
2NTST ↑64 Watch$19.64 +1.11%LOW2.1526.9%·
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) formed in the last 3 bars
  • LONG direction: excess on the LOW side AND trade location ≤ 30%
  • Universe: last_close > 0, max 500 tickers
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Two resolved trades is not a sample - the 100% win rate and +7.31% avg return vs SPY +1.16% over 20 days are directionally encouraging but carry no statistical weight, and the WATCH verdict is the correct read until the resolved count clears 20.

Data confidence STARTER prod-cabled KVO+FORCE combo, paper KEPT WR 67% vs SKIPPED 46% over 60d; no formal Bonferroni verdict yet Full methodology →
🧠 Filters SMART_FLOW signals by excluding trades where KVO or Force Index shows conflicting volume momentum within a 3-day window.
Construction
SMART_FLOW signals filtered by skipping any trade with KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS or FORCE_INDEX_FLIP active ±3 days. Volume rules act as a negative filter (combo OR-skip).
Top picks: $TLT $COIN
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1TLT ↑55 Watch$86.19 +0.11%BULLISH SWEEPCALL75$605KNEUTRAL0100STRONG1111
2COIN ↑50 Watch$159.02 -3.53%BULLISH SWEEPCALL182.5$225KOPENING138876BROKEN44
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads SMART_FLOW signals (2-day lookback)
  • Negative filter: SKIP if KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS OR FORCE_INDEX_FLIP volume rule active ±3 trading days
  • T+1 OI Delta filter: SKIP if call_volume(T+1)/call_volume(T) < 0.50
  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters
Score
original SMART_FLOW score, unchanged (no re-scoring). LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is thin - one name, COIN, scoring 50 (Watch tier) on a BULLISH SWEEP pattern. A single signal at the floor score with no entries reaching 65 or 80 is not a batch worth pressing. The score distribution is as concentrated as it gets: median equals max at 50, meaning no differentiation exists today. The macro regime sits at NEUTRAL (51.95), which neither supports nor kills a crypto-adjacent long, but it does nothing to lift conviction. COIN is the only option on the board so it gets first look by default, but at Watch tier and a score of 50, sizing should be minimal and patience is the right posture.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking contradicts the backtest REJECT: 470 resolved trades show a 53.6% win rate and avg +6.7%, beating SPY by 5.5 points, and the April-May cohorts (avg +12.7% and +7.9% respectively) confirm the edge is strengthening, but the Watch tier's -5.4% average is the one number that enforces strict tier discipline.

So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

MUU is the only name worth prioritizing today, scoring a perfect 100 in the Very strong tier - the sole stock above 80 in a 38-signal batch where the median score sits at 17. AGL at 67 (Signal tier) is a distant second and borderline given the NEUTRAL macro backdrop (regime score 51.95), which provides no tailwind for momentum breakouts. Everything below AGL is Watch-tier garbage scoring 29-53, where live tracking shows a 38.5% win rate and average loss of -5.4%, so ignore INTW, MVLL, KORU, SOXL, CRWG, and AMDL entirely. Score distribution is severely top-heavy: two names above 65, one above 80, and the rest clustered at the bottom. MUU first at high conviction, AGL as a speculative secondary only if the tape confirms intraday.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking (WR 54%, avg +2.8%, beats SPY by +1.0 pts) superficially contradicts the rejected backtest, but the tier breakdown exposes the real story: the Signal tier drives the KEEP verdict while the Strong tier, the one producing today's loudest signal in AIFU, posts a 20% win rate and -20.8% avg, making the overall KEEP label misleading at the tail where it matters most.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

AIFU carries the only score at or above 80 (85, Strong, TRIGGER) and is the surface headliner, but the Strong tier in live tracking is a landmine: 10 resolved trades, 20% win rate, avg return of -20.8%, meaning the highest-conviction signals have been the worst performers. VECO and VELO both score 75 in the Signal tier (SOW ZONE), and Signal is the only tier worth owning: 61% win rate, avg +9.5% across 90 resolved names. AMAT at 70 is notable as a large-cap semi in a crowded screen otherwise full of micro and small caps. The score distribution is bunched, with 34 of 50 signals between 65 and 74 and exactly one name above 80, which reads as a broad, undifferentiated batch rather than a high-conviction setup cluster. Macro regime is NEUTRAL at 51.95, offering no directional tailwind for shorts. First preference is VECO at low conviction, skipping AIFU entirely given Strong tier's documented failure rate.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is useless for discrimination: all 50 names print 99 or 100, median 99, so the ranker is saturating rather than ranking. Among the flagged names, RIOT stands out as a high-beta crypto proxy and OSCR as a volatile health insurer, both of which would amplify any directional call, for better or worse. PPLT and PALL are commodity ETFs, odd entries for a momentum equity ranker, and their presence with null patterns raises questions about universe contamination. Macro is NEUTRAL at 51.95, providing no tailwind or headwind read. Given a REJECTED backtest (alpha -0.8%/yr, p=0.25), a fully compressed score deck, and zero pattern context on any name, this batch is weak - no actionable preference today, watch only.

🧠 Flags US equities where weekly RSI(14) drops below 30 then triggers a daily RSI crossover plus candlestick reversal, targeting mean reversion off hard oversold readings.
Construction
Contrarian RSI(14) reversal: HARD GATE weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (coded 2026-06-09), then daily RSI oversold/crossover + candlestick reversal + demethod zone scoring.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 73% win rate is misleading: average return of +4.7% trails SPY by 2.5 points, the monthly cohort avg collapsed from +6.6% in March to +0.64% in May, and both numbers confirm the KILL verdict rather than argue against it.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

XPEV leads the batch at 70 (Signal, SETUP), the only name above 68 and the sole candidate worth prioritizing. BRR and BSY both sit at 68 Signal and are the next logical look, though they offer nothing mechanically distinct from XPEV. Score distribution is compressed and soft: median 53, nothing clears 80, only five names clear 65, making this a thin batch by any measure. A NEUTRAL macro regime (51.95) neither supports nor blocks a mean-reversion trade, but it provides no external catalyst to bail out a weak setup. Given the KILL verdict on this screener and the deteriorating May cohort, XPEV is the first look only, Signal tier, sized small, no conviction extension.

🧠 Detects unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs via volume spike, premium shift, and OI delta, weighted by spot crypto correlation to flag institutional positioning.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The headline 90-day KEEP verdict (65% WR, avg +2.0%, beats SPY by 1.6 pts) is entirely a Watch-tier artifact (73% WR, avg +5.3%, n=52), while the Signal tier runs at 14.3% WR and avg -20.3% across 7 resolved trades, directly contradicting the backtest rejection and flagging that the screener's higher-conviction calls are where the losses concentrate.

IREN is the only Strong-tier name at 80 with a TRIGGER pattern, sitting above a cluster of four names scored 70 or below. CLSK, HUT, and MARA all print 70 (Signal, BUY), while RIOT trails at 60 (Watch). The score distribution is compressed - median 70, only one name at 80 - which is not a high-conviction batch. Macro is NEUTRAL at 51.95, adding no tailwind. The critical problem: 90-day tier data shows Strong (0% WR, avg -13.2%, n=1) and Signal (14.3% WR, avg -20.3%, n=7) are the two worst-performing tiers, meaning the top four names today sit in historically losing buckets. If forced to act, RIOT at Watch is the only tier with a live-data edge; IREN at low conviction, not first.

ARKG leads at 72 and QCLN sits at 70, both Signal tier, making genomics and clean energy the two names worth watching first, though neither clears 75 and no pattern data sharpens the entry. BLOK, AIQ, and ROBO cluster at 67-68, rounding out the only five names above 65 in a 50-ticker universe where the median is 58 and nothing touches 80. That score compression tells you the batch is weak, no dominant conviction name, more a broad low-grade lift than a sharp theme breakout. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 51.95 neither helps nor hurts, but it removes the tailwind you would want before pressing a theme basket. If forced to act, ARKG first at low size, treating the 72 score as a watch entry, not a full position.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Eight names hit the perfect 100 score today, all tagged Very strong: BL, CELH, HDV, MAZE, QDEL, RNA, SRTY, and TNDM. SRTY is a 3x inverse Russell 2000 ETF sitting inside a long screener, which is the first thing to interrogate before acting - either the screener is picking up momentum in the short-small-cap trade or this is a mechanical artifact. CELH and BL are cleaner LONG candidates given their single-name, directional nature. Score distribution is top-heavy: 8 names at the 100 ceiling, median at 70 across 50 signals, and 41 of 50 above 65, so the field is broadly elevated rather than tightly clustered at the extremes. Macro regime sits at NEUTRAL (51.95), which does not confirm or block long setups but adds no tailwind. Pattern data is null across all picks, limiting signal specificity - lead with CELH on a pullback entry given the stock's history of momentum continuation, but size conservatively given no backtest and the SRTY anomaly in the batch.

🧠 Aggregates 13F filings from 20 top hedge funds, scoring new and increased positions to flag concentrated institutional conviction, with 60-90 day filing lag baked in.
Construction
Quarterly SEC 13F-HR filings from 20 top hedge funds via EDGAR. Whale Score: +20/NEW (cap 60), +10/INCREASED (cap 30), -5/CLOSED, -3/DECREASED. Stale 60-90 days by nature (quarterly).

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ASML at 97 and FB at 93 are the only two names in the Very strong tier, separating cleanly from a field where the median sits at 55 and just 13 of 50 signals clear 65. That distribution is top-heavy with a thin upper band, which narrows the actionable list fast. NEUTRAL macro at 51.95 adds no directional tailwind or headwind for these longs. No pattern data is attached to any name, so the entire case rests on score rank and the inference that multiple whales piled into the same names last quarter. ASML is the first look, highest score in the batch and a semiconductor name with clear institutional crowding logic; FB second given the 93 score, though the ticker tag appears stale relative to the current META listing.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

SVRE and LNBIX both print 100 and sit at the top of the list, but pattern fields are null across every name, leaving seniority-weighted dollar value as the only differentiator. HOOD at 98 stands out as the only name with meaningful liquidity and a recognizable institutional float, making it the most actionable if the underlying filing details confirm a C-suite buyer at size. The score distribution is nearly useless for ranking: all 50 signals clear 80 and the median is 94, meaning the screener is acting as a binary pass/fail filter rather than a gradient. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (51.95), which neither helps nor hurts insider-buy setups. Start with HOOD at moderate conviction given liquidity; treat SVRE and LNBIX as watch-list names until filing context is verified.

🧠 Ranks US equities by Tobias Carlisle's EV/EBIT ascending, surfacing the cheapest operating earners on a daily basis.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).
Top picks: $BZ $WRD $KROS

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The tape offers nothing usable: 0 of 21 picks have resolved, monthly cohorts are empty, and no win rate or SPY comparison exists, so the backtest verdict of NOT_YET_BACKTESTED and the 90-day WATCH status are both earned, not conservative.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
HALF SIZEinsufficient data (0 resolved, need ≥20)
Last 90 days: 21 signals captured
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 1 closed · WR TB 0% · 0% expired · sample too small to read (N<10)
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BZ at score 100 and WRD at 97 are the two names most worth examining, both landing in the Very strong tier as the cheapest operators in today's universe by Acquirer's Multiple. GPC at 90 is the most recognizable franchise in the batch and adds some quality weight to what is otherwise a names-you-have-to-look-up list. The score distribution is top-heavy: 7 of 16 signals clear 80 and 11 clear 65, so the batch is not thin, but no name carries a confirming pattern, which limits conviction. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (51.95), not a headwind but not a green light for deep value either. Start with BZ given its top rank, size it at low conviction until the screener earns a backtest.

🧠 Identifies stocks lagging their sector's established move, betting relative strength mean-reverts to close the intra-sector gap.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is weak and should be treated as such. All 12 signals, including the eight surfaced names (FUTU, BTGO, CRCL, LU, UWMC, PIPR, INTU, PAYP), score exactly 50 with zero clearing 65, which means the screener found technical laggards but no standout conviction differential. The score distribution is flat, not a power law, which undercuts the catch-up thesis entirely since you want one or two names pulling away from the pack, not a floor of identical mediocrity. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 51.95 gives no sector tailwind to accelerate the mean-reversion trade, making timing the catch-up move speculative at best. No actionable preference today - pass on the batch and wait for a signal above 65 before sizing into anything here.

🧠 Detects coordinated insider conviction by requiring 3+ Form 4 cluster buys within 30 days, weighting C-suite rank and total dollar commitment.
Construction
SEC Form 4 cluster buys: 3+ insiders buying within 30 days, with C-suite or director rank weighted higher. Score uses cluster size, total $ value, insider rank, and recency.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape at 62% win rate and +3.8% average versus SPY +1.7% supports the KEEP verdict, and the May 2026 cohort at 67% WR and +4.35% average confirms the trend is strengthening after April's weak 47% WR, making +2.1 points of SPY outperformance the key number to hold the screener accountable to going forward.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

All 16 signals today print at exactly 60, the screener floor, with zero names clearing 65 or 80, so there is no tier differentiation to exploit. FISV and MTDR stand out by size and sector relevance, FISV as a large-cap fintech where insider buys carry more dollar weight and MTDR as an energy name where insiders historically buy into cyclical troughs, but neither carries pattern confirmation today. The distribution is a flat stack, not a top-heavy conviction batch. NEUTRAL macro at 51.95 does not add tailwind. If forced to act, FISV first at low conviction, starter size only, given the absence of any score separation across the field.

The NEUTRAL macro regime (51.95) offers no directional lift, so broad ETF bull flow here carries less weight than it would in a risk-on tape. First name at highest conviction is IONQ - single-name, max score, sector with active thematic momentum - sized at moderate conviction until pattern data fills in.

🧠 Ranks tickers by 20-day relative strength vs SPY plus sector breadth and cluster position to surface momentum leaders inside bullish sectors.
Construction
Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.
Top picks: $AAT $AAMI $ABCL
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectorRet 1mRet 3m
1AAT ↑82●● Strong$24.39 +0.64%Real Estate19.0%31.5%
2AAMI ↑76● Signal$72.54 -1.83%Financials9.2%52.1%
3ABCL ↑74● Signal$5.67 -1.13%Healthcare7.8%47.3%
4A ↑65● Signal$127.92 +1.13%Healthcare15.9%17.1%
5ABCB ↑56 Watch$87.73 +0.42%Financials4.9%15.6%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=25 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags tickers where IV30 minus HV20 spikes above 1.5 sigma on 90-day history, surfacing candidates for options premium selling.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $NFLX $ALNY $CMCSA
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1NFLX ↓69● Signal$73.42 +0.74%46%27%1.73FEAR
2ALNY ↓61 Watch$290.68 +1.49%49%32%1.53FEAR
3CMCSA ↓61 Watch$22.40 +0.36%40%26%1.54FEAR
4NKE ↓61 Watch$43.00 -0.44%52%34%1.53FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms a live edge - 54.5% WR and +6.2% avg on 191 resolved trades beats SPY by 3.2 points, but the April-to-May cohort avg dropping from +10.8% to +3.7% is the key number, suggesting the premium-selling environment is cooling even as the verdict holds KEEP.

🧠 Flags retail-driven euphoria via social z-score, call/put spike, and price stretch without earnings catalyst to position SHORT into mean reversion.
Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.
Top picks: $ACN $BBIO $AVAV
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTierCall vol/OIChg
1ACN ↓100●●● Very strong$126.70 +1.50%FOMO1.05-3.0%
2BBIO ↓73● Signal$68.89 -0.04%GREED0.732.5%
3AVAV ↓52 Watch$149.16 -1.43%GREED0.52-5.6%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)
  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)
  • Filters: price > $10, options volume > 1000, total OI > 4000
Score
clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day resolved tape at 66% WR and +2.7% avg vs SPY's +0.43% supports keeping the screener, but the April cohort's 16.7% WR and -15.9% avg followed by May's 71.2% WR and +4.6% avg reveals extreme month-to-month dispersion that the aggregate number conceals, and with the backtest formally insufficient at N=17, the +2.3-point SPY edge is encouraging but not statistically confirmed.

NEUTRAL macro at 51.95 neither supports nor undermines a short bias. Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects spikes in 25-delta put/call skew vs 90-day baseline, reading elevated hedging demand as a contrarian buy signal when underlying price is calm.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $GOOGL $AVGO $ORCL
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1GOOGL ↓100●●● Very strong$344.20 -1.57%166%97—SELL CALL/PUT (IV rich)
2AVGO ↑58 Watch$382.87 -2.36%48%95-0.03BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
3ORCL ↑54 Watch$171.84 -1.84%53%680.00BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape broadly supports the screener with a 63% win rate and average gain of 14.1% against SPY's 2.4%, a spread of 11.7 points, but the tier breakdown is the number that matters: Very Strong and Strong signals are the worst performers in the cohort, averaging losses and sub-30% win rates, which directly contradicts the intuition that higher scores carry more edge.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=3 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags stocks where put IV overwhelms call IV at above the 90th percentile, signaling unusual directional or hedging put demand.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1AVGO ↑100●●● Very strong$382.87 -2.36%16.7468.7%97BEAR_SKEW_EXTREME
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Six resolved trades over 90 days show an 83% win rate but a 1.93% avg 20d return that trails SPY by 10 basis points, a gap driven entirely by one Strong-tier trade that lost 88.77% and cratered April's cohort avg to -20.2% - the Very strong and Signal tiers are each 100% win rate with 19-21% avg returns, confirming tier matters far more than the headline number, and the WATCH verdict is right given the sample is too small to call an edge real.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags pre-earnings names where straddle IV trades below Yang-Zhang HV20, suggesting the options market is underpricing realized volatility risk.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $MU
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1MU ↓50 Watch$1074.72 -11.28%REVERSAL1DOWN (reversal)BUY PUT (reversal)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is as thin as it gets: one name, ACN, scoring 50 on a Watch tier with no pattern tag. A score of 50 clears no meaningful threshold, zero signals reached 65 and zero reached 80, so the distribution is not clustered or top-heavy, it is simply absent. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (51.95), which does not actively oppose a vol-long setup but offers no tailwind either. With one sub-threshold, patternless signal in a flat regime, there is no actionable preference here. Pass on today's batch.

EOD
Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags closes outside the value area (VAL/VAH) that fully reverse next session, trapping directional traders and forcing a covering squeeze into the prior range.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $ALT
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR
1ALT ↑65● Signal$2.99 +2.05%BEAR_TRAP2.802.980.13
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The formal backtest holds N=0 so there is nothing to confirm or contradict, and the 90-day live tape of 7 resolved trades showing 85.71% win rate and a 17.04% average 20-day return against SPY's 0.73% reads encouraging but is statistically meaningless below the 20-trade minimum, a point the single May 2026 cohort underscores by offering no trend at all.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
HALF SIZEinsufficient data (7 resolved, need ≥20)
Last 90 days: 68 signals captured · 7 resolved at +20d · WR 86% · avg +16.5% · vs SPY +0.5%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 28 closed · WR TB 39% · 4% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 Detects fake breakouts above the Value Area High that close back inside, trapping long buyers and triggering a short signal on the reversal.
Construction
Fake breakout above VAH followed by a close back inside the value area = bull trap, longs trapped, SHORT signal.
Top picks: $AMBQ
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POC
1AMBQ ↓55 Watch$83.86 -5.31%BULL_TRAP91.5370.02
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BULL_TRAP (SHORT): close[N-1] > VAH then close[N] < VAH (failed bullish breakout rejected)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. SHORT.

Top picks: $VXX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankHV20Rule
1VXX ↓86●● Strong$23.86 +5.95%56%257%RULE_1
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Vol-decay basket (UVXY, VXX, SVXY, SDS, QID, SQQQ, FAZ, TZA)
  • Rule 1 (3 conditions required): IV30 < 100% AND IV Rank < 10 AND VIX3M/VIX > 1.0 (contango)
  • 2/3 conditions → inactive (score 55)
Score
if 3/3 active: min(100, 85 + max(0, 3−iv_rank)). NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape contradicts any constructive read: 17 resolved trades show a 23.5% win rate at an average 20-day return of -10.33% versus SPY's +0.32%, with May 2026 (n=16) posting -9.44% average and April's lone trade dropping -24.59%, so both cohorts trend in the same losing direction and the screener's WATCH verdict is well-earned.

Altman

STARTER · untested
no backtest stats yet
Refresh
EOD
Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 ALTMAN screens for financial distress risk using Altman Z-score derived factors to flag names with deteriorating solvency.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

The macro regime sits at NEUTRAL with a score of 51.95, so the tape is not the problem - the screener simply produced nothing actionable. No conviction warranted today; stand aside.

🧠 STFR screener logic is undocumented in this payload; construction details require inspection of the source script directly.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: price > $10 (shortable)
  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

🧠 VSA flags price-spread and volume divergences to identify institutional accumulation or distribution before a directional move.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Macro regime sits at NEUTRAL with a 51.95 score, which would support selective setups if any existed. No actionable preference is possible from a null batch.

Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.
Generated by build_screeners_deep.py · sources: signal_archive, whale_13f, openinsider, hrhr_ranked, options_flow_shortlist, emergence_history, sector_acceleration/laggards
Data confidence scale = a discretionary sizing guide (full / half / starter), never a permission verdict · stats from screener_validity + block-bootstrap v12
Sparklines = ~6 months daily close · AI reads regenerate at the EOD build, page refreshes hourly during US market hours
